wikitext:
[]: -             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Jul 29, 2008 9:29 am -

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 04, 2008 6:59 pm I though Prometheus got his sentence because of the whole stealing fire and then teaching humans to only offer the gods the crappy parts of animals as burnt sacrifices thing... but Greek myth has lots of contradictory tales depending on whose version you're reading, so no biggie. Also of note, technically there isn't much of a difference between gods and titans except for whether the current head of the pantheon likes you or not. It's more of a generational gap (though there is overlap) with political differences tossed in. then again, soem of the gods are personifications so abstract they never bother even taking a humanoid form, so any similarities in form are quite likley just a mater of personal choice... a very EVA-friendly concept. I was kinda thinking the evas would be named after various greek gods, most likely the principle triad that descended directly from Chronos (or, in one exception, from his actions) 00 = Poseidon 01 = Aphrodite 02 = Zeus 03 = Hades

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 04, 2008 9:05 pm If Gendo is Zues, than surely Unit-01 would be Hercules.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Aug 12, 2008 3:52 pm I can't believe I forgot about Oedipus! And his complex!

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Aug 12, 2008 3:53 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 12, 2008 4:35 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: I can't believe I forgot about Oedipus! And his complex!

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 12, 2008 6:37 pm ^ LOL ...especially because I imagined it as interpreted by random 40-something white guy in the form of a rap "to better reach the youth culture".

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Thu Aug 14, 2008 12:40 pm "...often represented as winged goddesses with serpent hair and eyes dripping blood, they relentlessly pursued their victims until the guilty died in furore of madness and remorse. So devastating was their power that the Greeks not speak their real name for fear of provoking their wrath; instead they employed the euphemistic term Eumenides: the kindly ones." These are the harpies, by the way. And there's the impenetrable Aegis shield, oh it's all coming back to me now.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Aug 14, 2008 12:47 pm NemZ wrote: as interpreted by random 40-something white guy in the form of a rap

[]: -             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Jul 29, 2008 9:29 am -

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 04, 2008 6:59 pm I though Prometheus got his sentence because of the whole stealing fire and then teaching humans to only offer the gods the crappy parts of animals as burnt sacrifices thing... but Greek myth has lots of contradictory tales depending on whose version you're reading, so no biggie. Also of note, technically there isn't much of a difference between gods and titans except for whether the current head of the pantheon likes you or not. It's more of a generational gap (though there is overlap) with political differences tossed in. then again, soem of the gods are personifications so abstract they never bother even taking a humanoid form, so any similarities in form are quite likley just a mater of personal choice... a very EVA-friendly concept. I was kinda thinking the evas would be named after various greek gods, most likely the principle triad that descended directly from Chronos (or, in one exception, from his actions) 00 = Poseidon 01 = Aphrodite 02 = Zeus 03 = Hades
